title: Savory Hearty Irish Lamb Stew
description: A rich and cozy Irish dish featuring fresh thyme and tender lamb, perfect for a cold winter's night, with anti-inflammatory properties from the thyme and other ingredients.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion, carrots, potatoes, celery, and garlic to the pot, cooking until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the lamb broth, thyme, salt, and pepper to the pot, stirring to combine.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Serve the stew hot, with slices of whole wheat bread on the side for dipping in the broth.
Let the stew cool slightly before serving, garnished with additional thyme if desired.
Toast the bread before serving, if desired, by placing it under the broiler for 1-2 minutes.
Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3 days.
